column1: # OWASP ZAP Baseline Scan — Rule Overrides
# Format: <rule-id>\t<IGNORE|WARN|FAIL>\t<justification>
# These rules are suppressed because they are CI-environment artefacts:
# the engine runs behind TLS/nginx in production, but the ZAP baseline
# scan targets raw uvicorn on HTTP (no TLS in CI).
# All active security controls (CSPMiddleware, SealGate, LicenseGate,
# RequestBodyLimitMiddleware) are tested by unit/integration tests.
# The ZAP scan produces an audit trail; it is not a hard gate.
# Rule IDs: https://www.zaproxy.org/docs/alerts/
# 10035 — Strict-Transport-Security Header Not Set
#         Production is served over TLS via nginx/Caddy. Raw uvicorn in CI
#         does not terminate TLS so HSTS cannot be present. Not a real finding.

column3: Production served over TLS; HSTS set by reverse proxy, not uvicorn

column1: # 10038 — Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Found (passive scan false positive)
#         CSPMiddleware is implemented and tested; ZAP may still flag if it
#         probes paths that return non-HTML responses where CSP is optional.

column3: CSPMiddleware enforced; flag if CSP absent on HTML endpoints

column1: # 10096 — Timestamp Disclosure - Unix
#         Timestamps in API responses are intentional (job created_at fields).

column3: Timestamps in JSON responses are intentional — not information disclosure

column1: # 10054 — Cookie Without SameSite Attribute
#         Engine uses token-based auth, not cookies. Any cookie set by uvicorn
#         test middleware is not part of the production auth flow.

column3: Engine uses token-based auth; cookies are not part of auth flow
